'''Chris Charles''' (born {{birth date|1976|03|07|df=y}}) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] (two spells), as a {{rlp|hk|sr}}, or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Charles made 231 appearances for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] between 1993 and 2002.<ref>{{cite web |title=200 Club – No 17 – Chris Charles |date=29 April 2021 |url=https://hullkr.co.uk/200-club-no-17-chris-charles/ |publisher=Hull Kingston Rovers |access-date=15 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220215153102/https://hullkr.co.uk/200-club-no-17-chris-charles/ | archive-date=15 February 2022}}</ref> He played for the club at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley]] in the 1997 Challenge Plate final.<ref>{{cite web |title=Chris Charles on highs and lows of Hull KR career and acrimonious exit |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/chris-charles-hull-kr-career-4206300 |website=HullLive |access-date=18 January 2026 |date=9 June 2020}}</ref>

===International honours===
Charles won a [[cap (sport)|cap]] for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Salford in 2005 against France.

==Personal life==
His son, [[Jack Charles (rugby league)|Jack Charles]], is a professional rugby league player who will play for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2026 and who played for cross-city rivals [[Hull F.C.]] between 2024 and 2025, as well as for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] on loan in the [[RFL Championship]] during 2025.<ref>{{cite news |title=Charles crosses city to join Hull KR |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2l82l0047o |access-date=24 October 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=24 October 2025}}</ref>
